title: Make a worm picker
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e90d3","text":"Make a worm picker. Platinum is used because it cools down very quickly after sterilisation under a flame.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"4SAyH","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"lMGNh","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"ACWA6","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":204707,"guid":"41ADD78B341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"10287 Platinum wire 0.2032mm (0.008in) dia For ISA Type R or S Standard Grade Thermocouple","mol_weight":0,"sku":"10287.G1","url":"https://www.alfa.com/en/catalog/010287/","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":243,"name":"Alfa Aesar","link":"https://www.alfa.com/en/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":204708,"guid":"41ADD7DF341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Holders for inoculating wire or needles up to Ø 056 mm","mol_weight":0,"sku":"459-0049","url":"https://uk.vwr.com/store/product?keyword=459-0049","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":1914,"name":"VWR International (Avantor)","link":"https://www.vwr.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Stick the platinum wire into the loop holder and screw the loop holder.
With your nails, flatten the end of the platinum wire. This is so as to make it into a bit of a spoon to collect the worms.
Your worm picker is ready! Sterilise under the flame before picking worms.
creator: Cristian Riccio
